page: Amazon Web Services m5n.12xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with x86_64 architecture, offering 48 vCPUs across 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at 3.1 GHz and includes 35 MB of L3 cache, alongside support for AVX-512 and AVX512_VNNI vector extensions. The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage.

In benchmark evaluations, the m5n.12xlarge demonstrates average multi-core performance, as evidenced by its mid-tier results in stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU tests. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, falling into the bottom 25% for stress-ng single-core and single-threaded gzip compression. The instance features a high baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps, which supports its positioning as a network-optimized server. Memory bandwidth tests for both cached and uncached reads and writes fall consistently within the average range.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m5n.12xlarge offers a high-density resource profile suitable for network-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. The combination of 50 Gbps network throughput and balanced compute-to-memory ratios makes it well-suited for database operations, multi-threaded data processing, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks. It is less optimal for single-threaded applications or workloads requiring high-performance local storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services m5n.12xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 192.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 instance delivers a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per vCPU. It lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on network-attached storage and offering a high baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, though single-core execution speeds are relatively weak. The instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for network-heavy, multi-threaded applications. It is best suited for workloads such as database operations, static web serving, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5n.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering average multi-core performance for database and web serving workloads.
